source.description: Six-panel contact sheet generated with the process-first workflow, then cropped into step frames and the finished image.
source.notes: Chosen after a quick event check because the FIFA World Cup is active in June-July 2026.

process_strategy: A six-panel process builds one soccer ball from a circle guide to pentagon/hexagon panel marks, grass tufts, shadow, and restrained graphite shading before the final keeper-line pass.

final_elements.name: round soccer ball outline
final_elements.description: A near-circle ball shape kept centered throughout the process.

final_elements.name: center pentagon
final_elements.description: A dark central pentagon that anchors the soccer panel pattern.

final_elements.name: radiating seam lines
final_elements.description: Curved seam lines reaching from the center pentagon toward the outer ball.

final_elements.name: outer panel shapes
final_elements.description: Surrounding pentagon and hexagon-like panels that complete the soccer ball pattern.

final_elements.name: grass tufts
final_elements.description: Short loose grass marks tucked under and around the ball.

final_elements.name: soft cast shadow
final_elements.description: A pale ground shadow that sits below the ball.

final_elements.name: restrained graphite shading
final_elements.description: Light pencil shading on selected dark panels and the underside of the ball.

frames.visible_job: Places a light ball circle, center guide, and ground line.
Adds the central pentagon and lightly marks its five corners.
Draws curved seam lines from the pentagon toward the ball edge.
Adds the outer panel shapes around the seam lines.
Adds grass tufts, a soft cast shadow, and light graphite shading.

final_step.visible_job: Only darkens existing panels, seams, grass, shadow, and graphite tone without adding new objects.

rejection_checks: No new panel system, grass cluster, prop, or color choice should first appear in the final image.
The ball must keep the same circle, scale, center pentagon, and seam layout in every frame.
The finish must read as a handmade pencil sketch, not a clean vector sports icon.
